title: India’s TakeMe2Space sets sights on 50-kilowatt data center
summary: Indian startup TakeMe2Space aims to raise $55 million to build a 50-kilowatt orbital data center after securing a $5 million seed round earlier this year. The company seeks to demonstrate its capability in the global orbital data center market.

title: Countering missile threats ‘left of launch’
summary: U.S. government agencies are collaborating with industry to develop pre-launch missile disruption tools under the 'left of launch' strategy. Raytheon Intelligence and Space is contributing expertise in space intelligence and surveillance.

title: Taiwan floats shared satcom constellation amid calls for more space collaboration
summary: Taiwan's space agency chief proposed a shared satellite communications constellation to compete with networks like Starlink. The initiative aims to enhance space collaboration among countries.

title: Companies make the case for commercial space station markets
summary: Companies developing commercial space stations argue that a market for such stations already exists, countering NASA's claims that the market has not yet developed.

title: Boeing and its subsidiary Millennium Space team on new mid-size satellite
summary: Boeing and its subsidiary Millennium Space are developing a new mid-size satellite platform to compete in the emerging 'micro GEO' market for space-based communications and sensing.

title: All Points signs agreement to build payload processing facilities at KSC
summary: All Points Logistics has signed an agreement with NASA’s Kennedy Space Center to build satellite processing facilities on center property. This collaboration aims to enhance payload processing capabilities for future space missions.

title: How space weather forecasting keeps astronauts (and satellites) safe
summary: The Space Weather Prediction Center (SWPC) in Boulder, Colorado, part of NOAA, works to protect astronauts and satellites from harmful space weather events like solar storms and flares. This effort is crucial for missions such as Artemis 2 and the safety of orbital assets.

title: NASA seeks commercial and exploration links for new Earth science missions
summary: NASA announced two new Earth science mission concepts, EAGLE and FALCON, at the 'Ignition' event, aiming to leverage commercial and exploration capabilities for key research topics.

title: The optimism in the space economy with Alyssa Goessler
summary: Alyssa Goessler discusses the optimism in the space economy on Space Minds, emphasizing the rigor required to evaluate space companies. The conversation highlights key drivers behind the growing confidence in the sector.

title: Artemis 2 is a benchmark in our space exploration program — and it depends on steady NASA funding
summary: The successful completion of Artemis 2 marks a significant milestone in NASA's space exploration program, but its future progress hinges on consistent funding. The mission's success has sparked discussions about the affordability and necessity of human spaceflight.

title: Landing astronauts on the moon 'is absolutely doable, and it's doable soon,' NASA's Artemis 2 commander says
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 commander stated that landing astronauts on the moon is achievable soon, following insights gained from their Orion capsule mission. The team's experience suggests positive prospects for upcoming Artemis flights.

title: 'That was a wonderful toilet': Artemis 2 commander defends mission's lunar loo
summary: Artemis 2 commander Reid Wiseman defended the mission's new space toilet, calling it 'wonderful' despite recent criticism. The toilet has faced negative attention over the past two weeks.

title: Fantasy RPG 'Crimson Desert' lets you stab your way up to space, showcasing a remarkably detailed cosmos
summary: The fantasy RPG 'Crimson Desert' features a unique gameplay mechanic allowing players to 'stab their way up to the edge of space'. The game showcases a highly detailed and imaginative cosmos.

title: Blue Origin fires up used New Glenn rocket ahead of landmark reflight (photo)
summary: Blue Origin successfully conducted a static fire test with a reused New Glenn rocket booster on April 16, setting the stage for its first reflight mission scheduled for April 19. This marks a significant milestone in reusable rocket technology.

title: The Lyrid meteor shower 2026 has begun! Here's what to expect
summary: The Lyrid meteor shower of 2026 has begun, with fireballs and meteors visible when the constellation Lyra is above the horizon.

title: This may be the daughter of one of our universe's 1st stars. Scientists call it an 'ancient immigrant'
summary: Scientists have identified a star believed to be a remnant from one of the universe's first stars, dubbed an 'ancient immigrant' as it moves from the Large Magellanic Cloud into the Milky Way.

title: Private Japanese spacecraft will inspect 2 dead satellites in 2027
summary: Astroscale's 2027 ISSA-J mission will inspect two dead satellites, marking the first attempt to examine multiple spacecraft in different orbits during a single flight.

title: 3D dark energy map is mind-blowing | Space photo of the day for April 16, 2026
summary: A new 3D dark energy map showcases individual galaxies as tiny points, offering unprecedented insights into the universe's expansion. The visualization, featured as the space photo of the day for April 16, 2026, highlights the vast scale of cosmic structures.

title: How to see once-in-a-lifetime Comet PanSTARRS tonight before it disappears for good
summary: Comet C/2025 R3 PanSTARRS is approaching its perihelion, offering a rare viewing opportunity before it disappears. The comet's close pass of the sun will be visible tonight.

title: Max Space unveils new expandable space habitat for the moon and beyond: 'We need real estate that is scalable'
summary: Max Space introduced a new expandable space habitat designed for lunar and deep-space missions, emphasizing scalability for long-duration flights and lunar habitation.

title: NASA Invites Media to Latvia Artemis Accords Signing Ceremony
summary: Latvia is set to sign the Artemis Accords during a ceremony at NASA Headquarters in Washington, hosted by NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman and Latvia’s minister for education and science, Dace Melbārde.

title: At the Edge of Light
summary: A photograph taken on April 6, 2026, captures the Moon's far side along the terminator, highlighting the Orientale Basin with long shadows from low-angle sunlight.

title: Eyeing the Richat Structure
summary: The Richat Structure, a circular geologic feature in northwestern Africa, is easily recognizable from space despite being hard to discern from the ground. This natural phenomenon has drawn interest for its unique appearance and geological significance.

title: I Am Artemis: Rebekah Tolatovicz
summary: Rebekah Tolatovicz, a mechanical technician lead, discusses her role in building, integrating, and testing Orion spacecraft for NASA's Artemis missions at Kennedy Space Center, working under Lockheed Martin as the main contractor.

title: NASA Selects Voyager for Seventh Private Mission to Space Station
summary: NASA and Voyager Technologies have signed an order for the seventh private astronaut mission to the International Space Station, targeting a launch no earlier than 2028 from Florida. This marks Voyager Technologies' first selection for such a mission, highlighting NASA's commitment to commercial space economy growth.

title: NASA Launches Six CubeSats to International Space Station
summary: NASA launched six CubeSats to the International Space Station as part of the Northrop Grumman Commercial Resupply Services 24 mission on April 11. The Cygnus XL spacecraft carried approximately 11,000 pounds of cargo, including supplies and experiments for the ISS.

title: 2026 NSTA Hyperwall Schedule
summary: NASA announced its schedule for the NSTA Hyperwall event in 2026, featuring presentations by NASA experts on topics like space weather and Artemis missions. The event will take place from April 16-18, 2026, at the Exhibit Hall (Booth #1265).

title: NASA’s SPHEREx Mission Maps Water Ice Throughout Cygnus X
summary: NASA's SPHEREx mission has mapped water ice and polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons in Cygnus X, a highly active star-forming region in the Milky Way. The observation highlights the distribution of these chemicals using spectral data.

title: ‘Interstellar Glaciers’: NASA’s SPHEREx Maps Vast Galactic Ice Regions
summary: NASA's SPHEREx mission has mapped vast interstellar ice regions in the Milky Way galaxy, covering areas over 600 light-years across. The ice was discovered within giant molecular clouds, which are dense regions of gas and dust.

title: Artemis II Crew Returns to Houston
summary: NASA's Artemis II crew returned to Houston after landing at Ellington Airport, where they shared brief remarks with friends, family, and colleagues. The crew includes NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and CSA astronaut Jeremy Hansen.

title: Expedition 74 sees vehicle departures and a spacewalk during March
summary: Expedition 74 crew conducted vehicle departures and a spacewalk during March. The activities highlight ongoing operations aboard the International Space Station.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to launch Starlink satellites, New Glenn to launch with flown booster
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 is set to launch Starlink satellites, while Blue Origin's New Glenn will launch with a previously flown booster. Six launches are scheduled worldwide this week.

title: Ship 39 Completes Static fire ahead of Booster 19 test Wednesday
summary: Ship 39 completed a static fire test in preparation for the Booster 19 test scheduled for Wednesday. This follows the successful return of Orion, marking a shift in focus for the Artemis program.

title: Falcon 9 launches CRS NG-24 cargo ship to ISS
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 successfully launched the CRS NG-24 cargo mission to the International Space Station (ISS). This follows Northrop Grumman's recent completion of the CRS NG-23 mission with its Cygnus spacecraft.

title: Artemis II returns to Earth after historic 10-day mission around the Moon
summary: Artemis II completed a historic 10-day mission around the Moon, traveling over 800,000 km before returning to Earth. This marks a significant milestone in NASA's lunar exploration program.

title: Artemis II closing in on Earth return
summary: Artemis II is nearing its return to Earth after a historic 10-day mission that took humans farther from Earth than any previous crewed mission. The mission marks a significant milestone in NASA's Artemis program.

title: China debuts new launchers, tests orbital servicing and outlines future deep-space missions
summary: China has debuted new commercial launchers from CAS Space and Space Pioneer, while also testing orbital servicing and outlining future deep-space missions.

title: SpaceX continues refinements at Starbase Pad 2 ahead of Booster 19 return
summary: SpaceX is making final preparations at its Starbase Pad 2 for the return of Booster 19. The refinements aim to ensure readiness for upcoming operations at the facility.

title: Artemis II breaks record, conducts lunar flyby
summary: Artemis II achieved a record-breaking lunar flyby with the Orion spacecraft Integrity, carrying astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen. The mission marks a significant milestone in NASA's Artemis program.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to loft Cygnus to ISS, Minotaur IV to launch from California
summary: A Falcon 9 rocket is set to launch a Cygnus spacecraft to the International Space Station, while a Minotaur IV rocket is scheduled to launch from California. These launches follow the halfway point of NASA's Artemis II mission.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website is mentioned without specific details in the provided news snippet.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. The publication celebrates key milestones and contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual SME Forum 2026, which includes an online preparatory session in April-May and an in-person event at ESA Headquarters in Paris on June 8-9, 2026. The forum aims to gather feedback from smaller enterprises on topics affecting the space sector.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: The article discusses the successful transmission of Celeste's first navigation signal. It highlights the significance of this milestone for the space industry.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has conducted 57 artificial solar eclipses and collected over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona data since July 2025. The mission reveals solar wind structures in the inner corona travel 3-4 times faster than previously thought.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is open for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026, scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency’s technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Pointing in the (b)right direction
summary: The article discusses advancements in spacecraft pointing technology, likely focusing on improved precision or new methods for directional control. This innovation could enhance mission accuracy and efficiency in space operations.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team demonstrated light propulsion using graphene aerogels in microgravity during ESA's parabolic flight campaign in May 2025. The study, led by Université Libre de Bruxelles and Khalifa University, showed that lasers can propel these materials with greater efficiency in space-like conditions.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: Graphene and lasers could revolutionize space propulsion by steering solar sails and adjusting satellite positions. An experiment simulating zero gravity demonstrated the potential of this technology.

title: A silicon chip that can do it all
summary: Researchers have developed a silicon chip capable of performing multiple functions, potentially revolutionizing space-based computing. This advancement could enhance the efficiency and capabilities of satellites and spacecraft.

title: Voyager Technologies Wins Its First Private ISS Mission
summary: Voyager Technologies has secured its first private mission to the International Space Station (ISS) in 2026, marking a shift as new players enter the competition previously dominated by Axiom Space.

title: Seraphim Space Aims To Shape Strategy With New Advisory Council
summary: Seraphim Space has established a new advisory council to address key challenges in the space industry, including sustainability, regulatory issues, national security, and international collaboration.

title: ORBX ETF Launches Amid Wave of Space Tech IPOs
summary: The ORBX ETF has been launched to track companies generating over half their revenue from space technology, including transportation, software, and satellite data services. This launch follows a trend of increasing space tech IPOs.

title: Kepler Leads Team to Test ESA’s ‘Fiber in the Sky’
summary: ESA has selected a team led by Kepler Communications to test the agency's 'fiber in the sky' HydRON orbital communications network. The initiative aims to demonstrate advanced space-based data transmission capabilities.

title: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman On Artemis, Budget, And Establishing a Lasting Space Vision
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman discusses the Artemis program, budget challenges, and the need for a sustainable space vision. He highlights the passion and scientific potential of the space industry while questioning the financial planning behind major projects.

title: CLD Companies Say NASA Is Wrong. NASA Says Prove It.
summary: Commercial Low Earth Orbit (CLD) companies dispute NASA's claims, while NASA challenges them to prove their objections wrong. The exchange occurred during the Space Symposium.

title: Slingshot Introduces AI-Powered Portal Platform
summary: Slingshot Aerospace launched an AI-powered portal platform to help operators detect anomalies and make decisions in a unified interface. The platform aims to streamline space operations through advanced artificial intelligence.

title: ICON Prime Launches, Led By Former TX Rep
summary: ICON Prime has launched as a new business unit focused on working with the Pentagon and NASA, led by a former Texas representative. The robotic construction company ICON aims to expand its operations into space-related projects through this initiative.

title: Exclusive: Vast Debuts Flight Suit For Haven-1, Private Astronaut Missions
summary: Vast has unveiled a new flight suit designed for its upcoming Haven-1 space station and private astronaut missions. The company aims to modernize astronaut apparel with a stylish and functional design.

title: Atomic-6 Launches Orbital Data Center Marketplace
summary: Atomic-6, a GA-based composite manufacturer, has launched ODC.Space, a marketplace to simplify securing orbital compute. This initiative aims to accelerate the growth of the orbital data center market.

title: Space Force, NATO Commanders Share Threat Perspectives
summary: U.S. Space Force is identifying new space capabilities and monitoring threats from adversaries while creating missions to defend assets. NATO commanders share perspectives on space security threats.

title: Air Force Awarded Development Contract for Space-Based AMTI; Meink Says Risk Low
summary: The U.S. Department of the Air Force awarded a contract to multiple vendors for the development of space-based air moving target indication (AMTI). The move is seen as low-risk by officials.
company: U.S. Department of the Air Force

title: Boeing and Millennium Space Systems Add Mid-Class Resolute Satellite Bus
summary: Boeing and its subsidiary Millennium Space Systems have introduced a new mid-class satellite platform called Resolute, offering enhanced capabilities compared to traditional small satellites.

title: Slingshot Aerospace Releases AI-Powered Analytics Platform for Space Operations
summary: Slingshot Aerospace launched Portal, an AI-powered analytics platform for satellite data monitoring and analysis. The platform integrates Slingshot's data flows with external sources to enhance space operations.

title: Industry Builds the Case Around Architecture Options for Orbital Data Centers
summary: Reductions in satellite launch prices and demand for alternatives to terrestrial data centers are driving interest in developing orbital space data centers. The industry is exploring architecture options to support this emerging market.

title: Transport Layer of PWSA Will Eventually Transition From SDA to New Acquisition Executive, Sandhoo Says
summary: The Space Force plans to transfer the communications layer of the SDA's PWSA to a new acquisition executive. This transition is part of the Space Force's updated acquisition structure.

title: COMSO Targets Expanding Working Capital Fund and Determining CASR Funding
summary: Space Systems Command’s Commercial Space Office (COMSO) is aiming to expand its Working Capital Fund and determine funding for the Commercial Assessment of Space Resiliency (CASR). The initiative focuses on leveraging commercial space capabilities.

title: Teledyne Space Imaging President Megan Tremer Shares Enabling Tech for Artemis II
summary: Teledyne Space Imaging President Megan Tremer discussed the technologies provided by Teledyne for the Artemis II mission in an interview with Via Satellite during the Space Symposium.

title: Moog Space SVP Mark Covelli Details Meteor Satellite Bus at Space Symposium
summary: Moog Space's senior vice president Mark Covelli discussed the company's support for the Artemis II mission and its investment in the Meteor satellite bus during the Space Symposium.

title: Lonestar Expands Launch Plans for Orbital Data Storage Payload
summary: Lonestar announced the launch date for StarVault, the world's first commercially operational space-based sovereign data storage platform. The company highlighted growing demand for off-planet data storage solutions.
